statusLine: European fuel security is under pressure with 13 of 15 EU countries below minimum stock requirements amid rising oil prices and regional supply disruptions.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel markets are experiencing significant strain as the majority of monitored EU countries fall short of mandatory 90-day stock requirements. Romania faces the most severe situation with critically low reserves across all fuel types—just 43.6 days of petrol, 20.2 days of diesel, and 60 days of jet fuel. Poland and Spain also show critical levels across all categories, while even major economies like Germany and France struggle with diesel stocks at 63.9 and 83.4 days respectively.

Diesel supplies present the greatest concern, with 11 of 15 countries below the 90-day threshold despite an EU average of 95.8 days. This reflects the heavy reliance on diesel across European transport and industry. Jet fuel reserves are similarly tight, with 13 countries in critical or warning status, averaging just 89.9 days EU-wide.

The supply pressures coincide with Brent crude trading at $111.21 per barrel, up 1.31% and approximately 27% higher since the current Middle East conflict began. Regional fuel price disparities are stark, with Netherlands petrol at €2.334 per liter compared to Malta's €1.34. These elevated prices, combined with depleted strategic reserves released during recent supply disruptions, highlight the vulnerability of European energy security to geopolitical shocks.
keyPoints: 13 of 15 monitored EU countries fall below the mandatory 90-day minimum stock requirement for at least one fuel type
Romania shows the most critical situation with diesel reserves at just 20.2 days, well below emergency thresholds
Diesel supplies are most constrained, with 11 countries below 90-day requirements despite adequate EU-wide averages
Brent crude at $111.21 per barrel represents a 27% increase since Middle East supply disruptions began
Major economies including Germany, France, and Italy face critical diesel shortages despite adequate petrol reserves
